title: ALL nodes with cached value push to every new subscriber
symptom: Subscriber receives initial DATA immediately on subscribe
cause: Spec v0.2: every node with cached value (not SENTINEL) pushes [[DATA, cached]] to each new subscriber. This replaces the v0.1 eager-compute model. Applies to state, derived, producer — any node kind.
pattern: Node with cached value → subscribe → sink receives [[DATA, cached]]. SENTINEL (no initial) → no push. 1st sub triggers activation (upstream wiring / producer start); 2nd+ sub still gets cached push.

title: SENTINEL nodes do not push on subscribe
symptom: Derived node depending on SENTINEL dep never computes
cause: Node created without initial option has cache=SENTINEL. No value to push. Derived stays disconnected.
fix: Use state(undefined) or state(null) if the dep should have an initial value. Use node() without initial only when 'no value yet' is the correct semantic.

title: Wire observers before producers for streaming sources
symptom: Observer misses messages from producer/stream source
cause: Producers and streaming sources are fire-and-forget. Late subscribers miss past emissions. Any node with cached value pushes to late subscribers — ordering only matters for streaming/producer sources.
fix: For state/derived deps: subscribe anytime (cached value pushed to every new subscriber). For producer/stream deps: subscribe before the source starts emitting.
escape_hatch: TopicGraph.retained() returns buffered entries for catch-up

title: Guard null/undefined in effect fns only when null is a valid domain value
symptom: Effect fn throws or processes null when deps hold initial null value
cause: State(null) pushes [[DATA, null]] on subscribe. Effect fires immediately with null.
fix: When null is a valid domain value: add 'if (val != null) return;' (loose equality catches both null and undefined). When 'no value yet' is the intent: use node() without initial (SENTINEL) instead of state(null) — no guard needed since SENTINEL does not push.

title: ReactiveMapBundle wraps values in Versioned
symptom: Accessing map values requires navigating through Versioned wrapper
cause: Versioned wrapper exists for efficient RESOLVED dedup
fix: Use bundle.get(key) for single-key reads. Only navigate Versioned when using the node as a reactive dep

title: Wire graph factories in sink-to-source order
pattern: 1. Create sinks (TopicGraphs, state nodes) → 2. Create processors (derived/effect) → 3. Keepalive internal nodes → 4. Mount subgraphs → 5. Return controller
reason: Ensures that when stage N emits, stage N+1 is already wired to receive

title: Break feedback cycles with withLatestFrom, not sync .get()
cause: Downstream write feeds back to upstream reactive dep → triage re-fires → execute → verify → loop
fix: Use withLatestFrom(trigger, advisory) — trigger is the primary reactive source, advisory is sampled without triggering

title: promptNode gates on nullish deps and empty prompt text
symptom: LLM adapter called with empty/meaningless prompts on initial activation
cause: Deps push null/undefined initial values; prompt fn produces text from null
fix: promptNode internally checks: if any dep != null (loose equality, catches both null and undefined) → skip. If prompt text is falsy → skip. Return '' from prompt fn when input is meaningless. Prefer SENTINEL deps (node() without initial) over state(null) when 'no value yet' is the intent.

title: Mandatory debugging procedure for composed factories
symptom: OOM, infinite loops, silent failures, unexpected values
fix: 1. Re-read COMPOSITION-GUIDE. 2. Isolate the failing test. 3. Inspect with describe()/graphProfile. 4. Trace the reactive chain upstream. 5. Fix root cause not symptom.

title: Keepalive subscriptions activate computation chains
symptom: Derived node has no subscribers and never computes
cause: Derived nodes are lazy — they only subscribe to deps (triggering push cascade) when they have a downstream subscriber. First subscriber triggers _connectUpstream which wires to deps; deps then push cached values.
fix: Add keepalive: node.subscribe(() => {}). The empty sink triggers upstream wiring, which causes deps to push, which drives computation. Keepalive is just an empty sink — activation comes from the upstream connection it triggers.

title: LLM cost control
pattern: state (knob via meta) → derived chain → gauges via meta

title: Security policy enforcement
pattern: state + derived + effect with PAUSE propagation

title: Human-in-the-loop
pattern: Two state nodes (human + LLM) → derived gate → effect

title: Multi-agent routing
LLM builds graph from snapshot
Git-versioned graphs
Custom domain signals
pattern: User-defined message types + onMessage to intercept; unhandled types forward through graph

title: dynamicNode rewire buffer: lazy-dep stabilization
symptom: dynamicNode fn sees undefined for lazy deps on first run
cause: dynamicNode runs fn immediately (no pre-set dirty mask). Lazy deps are disconnected at fn time, so get() returns undefined. After _rewire subscribes to them, their activation push arrives with the real value.
fix: Rewire buffer detects DATA discrepancies and re-runs fn (bounded by MAX_RERUN=16). Identity check (_depValuesDifferFromTracked) prevents redundant re-runs when deferred handshake DATA matches what fn already saw.

title: ROM/RAM cache semantics: state retains, compute clears
symptom: get() returns undefined on disconnected derived/effect/dynamic node
cause: Compute nodes (derived, producer, effect, dynamic) clear cache on disconnect (RAM). State nodes retain cache (ROM).
fix: Always subscribe before reading compute node values. Call get() before unsub() in tests. Reconnect always re-runs fn from scratch.

title: startWith removed — use derived with initial
symptom: startWith is not found in operators
cause: First-run gate and START handshake make startWith unnecessary. Use derived([source], ([v]) => v, { initial }) instead.
fix: Replace startWith(source, value) with derived([source], ([v]) => v, { initial: value })
